description: In Zulu, prenasalization triggers several allophonic changes in the following consonant. The nasal assimilates in place of articulation and may cause voicing of following obstruents, creating harmonic clusters. This is an allophonic process affecting consonant sequences.
sources: Doke, C. M. (1931). _An Outline of Zulu Grammar_. Johannesburg: Witwatersrand University Press.
Maddieson, I. (2013). _The Sounds of the World's Languages_ (2nd ed.). Oxford: Wiley-Blackwell.
Canonici, N. N. (1998). _Imisindo YesiZulu: An Introduction to Zulu Phonology_. University of Natal Press.
